title: potentilla callieri
side_effects: Potentilla callieri can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting due to its high saponin content. Long-term use or excessive consumption may lead to liver damage and decreased blood clotting ability. In rare cases, allergic reactions like skin rashes, itching, and respiratory problems have been reported. Additionally, pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using Potentilla callieri due to its potential to stimulate uterine contractions and affect fetal development.
